title: Fix the default timestamps() — global upgrade
prompt: A project has dozens of schemas all using default `timestamps()` (naive_datetime). Write a migration that does NOT modify the existing schemas but instead establishes an app-wide convention. Create lib/my_app/schema.ex — a reusable `use MyApp.Schema` macro that imports Ecto.Schema, sets default `@primary_key {:id, :id, autogenerate: true}` and defaults timestamps to :utc_datetime via `Module.register_attribute(__MODULE__, :timestamps_opts, ...)`. Existing schemas should then `use MyApp.Schema` instead of `use Ecto.Schema`. Write the helper module to lib/my_app/schema.ex.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing @timestamps_opts and the __using__ macro pattern. Medium-to-hard because the indirection is non-obvious.
research_citation: Ecto Issue #2683 — maintainer acknowledgment that naive_datetime default is regrettable.
